question_text: A student pulls a 5 kg box across a wooden floor with a rope at an angle of 30° above the horizontal. The pulling force in the rope is 20 N. The coefficient of friction between the box and floor is 0.4. Calculate the friction force acting on the box. (Gravitational field strength g = 10 N/kg. Hint: resolve the pulling force into horizontal and vertical components first.)
explanation: First, find the normal force: The vertical component of the pulling force is 20 × sin(30°) = 10 N upward. Weight = 5 × 10 = 50 N downward. Normal force = 50 - 10 = 40 N. Then calculate friction: friction = coefficient of friction × normal force = 0.4 × 40 = 16 N. The angled pull reduces the normal force because part of it pushes upward, which decreases friction compared to pulling horizontally.
